Author: Akari
Name: Housing Lotto Tracker
Punchline: Track your housing lottery bids: plot, world, entry number, results date, win/loss.
Description:
Passively records every housing lottery bid you place, per character, by reading the placard when you interact with it.

- One row per active bid: character (with world), region, district, ward, plot, plot size, FC-vs-personal, entry date, results-available date, and outcome (Pending / Won / Lost).
- Auto-capture: reads the housing placard (HousingSignBoard) when you open it, during both the entry and results periods. No need to type anything.
- Entry number: grabbed automatically at bid time when the game shows it; left editable when it can't be read (the game does not persist your personal ticket number).
- Win detection: when a results placard congratulates you, the matching bid is flipped to Won automatically (reuses the same SelectYesno hook FC Tracker uses).
- Results countdown: each bid shows the results-available date (entry + 5 days) and a live countdown to when results open and when the claim/refund window closes.
- Sortable table with click-to-expand details, per-column visibility toggles, and editable notes.
- Optional shared storage so all clients on the same Windows user share one dataset, regardless of --roamingPath, with multi-client-safe merging.

Open with /hlt (alias /lotto).
